section_name: Use of State Money for High-speed Rail

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: For the purposes of this section, “high-speed rail” means intercity passenger rail service that is reasonably expected to reach speeds of at least 110 miles per hour.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Except as required by federal law or other state law, including the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (42 U.S.C. Section 4321 et seq.):

the legislature may not appropriate money to pay for a cost of planning, facility construction or maintenance, or security for, promotion of, or operation of, high-speed rail operated by a private entity; and
a state agency may not accept or use state money to pay for a cost described by Subdivision (1).

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: A state agency shall prepare a semiannual report of each expense described by this section and submit a copy of each report to:
the committee in each house of the legislature with primary jurisdiction over transportation;
the speaker of the house of representatives;

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: This section is not intended to preclude or limit the execution of the department’s responsibilities under federal law or other state law, including:
coordinating with a private entity that operates or proposes to operate high-speed rail in the same manner that the department coordinates with other entities that operate transportation projects.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 311 (S.B.977), Sec. 1, eff. September 1, 2017.
